title: Private Jet Crashes on Highway in Laredo, Texas, Killing 1
description: The plane’s crew reported mechanical problems and low fuel as they flew to Austin, Texas, from Mexico. Five of the six people on board survived the crash in Laredo.
author: Qasim Nauman

title: What to Know About Redistricting Efforts Underway in Georgia
description: Georgia is the latest Southern state where lawmakers have taken up redrawing congressional maps after the Supreme Court’s voting rights ruling.
author: Rick Rojas

title: Is Georgia Senator Jon Ossoff Running for President? He Has to Win Re-election First.
description: The 39-year-old senator has become an internet sensation for Democrats seeking a 2028 contender. He says he’s focused on winning a second term in November.
author: Reid J. Epstein and Patricia Mazzei

title: Seven Takeaways From the First World Cup Games at U.S. Venues
description: The crowds have been big, the fans rabid and the lines, well, long. Here’s a glimpse of the atmosphere at sites across the country as the tournament started.
author: Heather Knight and Jenna Russell

title: Illinois Town Loses a Prison, Leaving Residents Worried About Jobs
description: Lincoln, Ill., where Abraham Lincoln once worked in the local courthouse, has lost two colleges, a bottle factory and now a state prison.
author: Julie Bosman

title: The Cloud Has Sound: The Unrelenting and Unseen Cost of the A.I. Boom
description: As tech giants rush to build big data centers, some residents who live near them say a constant low-frequency vibration is ruining their health and homes.
author: Adeel Hassan

title: Wildfire in Washington State Prompts Evacuation Orders
description: Dry and windy conditions could exacerbate the blaze, which has already destroyed some structures near Spokane, officials warned.
author: Yan Zhuang

title: Georgia Vexes Trump Yet Again: 6 Takeaways From Tuesday’s Primaries
description: Lt. Gov. Burt Jones lost the Republican runoff for governor to the health care executive Rick Jackson despite the president’s endorsement. Mr. Trump’s picks won in other races.
author: Patricia Mazzei

title: The World Cup Gives Kansas City a Rare Turn in the Global Spotlight
description: The tournament brings unique challenges and upsides for Kansas City, the smallest U.S. metro area to host matches.
author: Mitch Smith

title: Here’s the latest.
author: Reid J. Epstein

title: Ohio Governor Says State Should End the Death Penalty, Breaking With His Party
description: During a news conference on Tuesday, Gov. Mike DeWine, a Republican, called on state lawmakers to end all executions, a practice he once supported.
author: Madaleine Rubin

title: Rep. Barry Moore Wins Senate Primary in Alabama, Holding Off Challenger
description: The contest to fill Alabama’s open Senate seat had become closer than expected between the House lawmaker and Jared Hudson, a political newcomer.
author: Emily Cochrane

title: At Least 5 Charged in Alleged Plot to Attack White House During U.F.C. Event
description: Charging documents outlined an ambitious plan involving explosive-laden drones and rifles, but left less clear that the conspirators had the means to carry it out.
author: Devlin Barrett

title: State Lawmaker and Trump-Backed Pastor Head to House Runoff in Oklahoma
description: The result sets up a Republican primary runoff between a right-wing firebrand and a more traditional candidate.
author: Bayliss Wagner and Tim Balk

title: Rick Jackson, a Billionaire, Wins G.O.P. Runoff for Georgia Governor
description: Mr. Jackson, who had been largely unknown to voters just months ago, beat President Trump’s choice and will face Keisha Lance Bottoms, a Democrat, in November.
author: Rick Rojas

title: Attorney General and Businessman Make Oklahoma Governor’s Runoff
description: Gentner Drummond, the attorney general, and Mike Mazzei, a newcomer who secured President Trump’s endorsement, emerged from a crowded field in the Republican primary.
author: J. David Goodman

title: One Dead and Another Hurt in Shooting at Hospital in Wilmington, Del.
description: The police have arrested a suspect, and an earlier lockdown at Wilmington Hospital has been lifted.
author: Neil Vigdor

title: Trump Officials Challenge Reparations Program in Chicago Suburb
description: The Justice Department argued that the program, which seeks to compensate Black residents for housing discrimination, was racist and unconstitutional.
author: Chris Cameron

title: Barn Fire Kills 17 Horses at Harness Track in Saratoga Springs, N.Y.
description: The cause is under investigation. Only one horse in the barn, Twin B Speed Dial, is believed to have survived, sustaining minor injuries.
author: Mack Liederman

title: As Mourning Begins, Deadly B-52 Crash Highlights Age of Bomber Fleet
description: Investigators are just beginning to determine what went wrong, but the Air Force has struggled to maintain the planes, which were first introduced in 1955.
author: Shawn Hubler, Dave Philipps and John Ismay
